# Copyright The Volcano Authors.

SHELL := /usr/bin/env bash -o pipefail
.SHELLFLAGS := -ec

HUB ?= ghcr.io/volcano-sh
TAG ?= latest
PROJECT_DIR := $(shell pwd)
GOBIN ?= $(shell go env GOBIN)
CONTAINER_TOOL ?= docker

LOCALBIN ?= $(PROJECT_DIR)/bin
CONTROLLER_GEN ?= $(LOCALBIN)/controller-gen
GOLANGCI_LINT ?= $(LOCALBIN)/golangci-lint

CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION ?= v0.17.2
GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION ?= v1.64.1

WORKLOAD_MANAGER_IMAGE ?= workloadmanager:latest
ROUTER_IMAGE ?= agentcube-router:latest
PICOD_IMAGE ?= picod:latest
IMAGE_REGISTRY ?= ""

E2E_CLUSTER_NAME ?= agentcube-e2e
AGENT_SANDBOX_REPO ?= https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox.git
AGENT_SANDBOX_VERSION ?= main

##@ General

.PHONY: all
all: build

.PHONY: help
help: ## Display this help.
	@awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*##"; printf "\nUsage:\n  make \033[36m<target>\033[0m\n"} /^[a-zA-Z_0-9-]+:.*?##/ { printf "  \033[36m%-15s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2 } /^##@/ { printf "\n\033[1m%s\033[0m\n", substr($$0, 5) } ' $(MAKEFILE_LIST)

##@ Development

.PHONY: generate
generate: controller-gen ## Generate code containing DeepCopy, DeepCopyInto, and DeepCopyObject method implementations.
	$(CONTROLLER_GEN) object:headerFile="hack/boilerplate.go.txt" paths="./pkg/apis/..."
	$(MAKE) gen-crd

.PHONY: gen-crd
gen-crd: controller-gen ## Generate CRD manifests.
	$(CONTROLLER_GEN) crd paths="./pkg/apis/runtime/v1alpha1/..." output:dir=manifests/charts/base/crds

.PHONY: gen-client
gen-client: ## Generate client code.
	bash hack/update-codegen.sh
	go mod tidy

.PHONY: gen-all
gen-all: generate gen-client ## Run all generators.

.PHONY: gen-check
gen-check: gen-all ## Check if generated code is up to date.
	git diff --exit-code

.PHONY: gen-copyright
gen-copyright: ## Generate copyright headers.
	bash hack/update-copyright.sh

##@ Build

.PHONY: build
build: generate ## Build workload manager binary.
	go build -o bin/workloadmanager ./cmd/workload-manager

.PHONY: build-agentd
build-agentd: generate ## Build agentd binary.
	go build -o bin/agentd ./cmd/agentd

.PHONY: build-router
build-router: generate ## Build router binary.
	go build -o bin/agentcube-router ./cmd/router

.PHONY: build-picod
build-picod: ## Build picod binary.
	go build -o bin/picod ./cmd/picod

.PHONY: build-all
build-all: build build-agentd build-router build-picod ## Build all binaries.

.PHONY: run
run: generate ## Run workload manager locally.
	go run ./cmd/workload-manager/main.go --port=8080 --runtime-class-name=

.PHONY: run-local
run-local: generate ## Run workload manager with local kubeconfig.
	go run ./cmd/workload-manager/main.go --port=8080 --runtime-class-name= --kubeconfig=${HOME}/.kube/config

.PHONY: run-router
run-router: generate ## Run router locally.
	go run ./cmd/router/main.go --port=8080 --debug

.PHONY: clean
clean: ## Clean build artifacts.
	rm -rf bin/
	rm -f workloadmanager agentcube-router picod agentd

##@ Dependencies

.PHONY: deps
deps: ## Download dependencies.
	go mod download
	go mod tidy

.PHONY: update-deps
update-deps: ## Update dependencies.
	go get -u ./...
	go mod tidy

##@ Test

.PHONY: test
test: ## Run unit tests.
	go test -v ./...

.PHONY: fmt
fmt: ## Format code.
	go fmt ./...

.PHONY: vet
vet: ## Run go vet.
	go vet ./...

.PHONY: lint
lint: golangci-lint ## Run golangci-lint.
	$(GOLANGCI_LINT) run ./...

##@ Docker

.PHONY: docker-build
docker-build: ## Build workload manager image.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) build -f docker/Dockerfile -t $(WORKLOAD_MANAGER_IMAGE) .

.PHONY: docker-build-router
docker-build-router: ## Build router image.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) build -f docker/Dockerfile.router -t $(ROUTER_IMAGE) .

.PHONY: docker-build-picod
docker-build-picod: ## Build picod image.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) build -f docker/Dockerfile.picod -t $(PICOD_IMAGE) .

.PHONY: docker-buildx
docker-buildx: ## Build workload manager image with buildx.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 -f docker/Dockerfile -t $(WORKLOAD_MANAGER_IMAGE) .

.PHONY: docker-buildx-router
docker-buildx-router: ## Build router image with buildx.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 -f docker/Dockerfile.router -t $(ROUTER_IMAGE) .

.PHONY: docker-buildx-picod
docker-buildx-picod: ## Build picod image with buildx.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 -f docker/Dockerfile.picod -t $(PICOD_IMAGE) .

.PHONY: docker-buildx-push
docker-buildx-push: ## Build and push workload manager image.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 --push -t $(IMAGE_REGISTRY)/$(WORKLOAD_MANAGER_IMAGE) -f docker/Dockerfile .

.PHONY: docker-buildx-push-router
docker-buildx-push-router: ## Build and push router image.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 --push -t $(IMAGE_REGISTRY)/$(ROUTER_IMAGE) -f docker/Dockerfile.router .

.PHONY: docker-buildx-push-picod
docker-buildx-push-picod: ## Build and push picod image.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) buildx build --platform linux/amd64,linux/arm64 --push -t $(IMAGE_REGISTRY)/$(PICOD_IMAGE) -f docker/Dockerfile.picod .

.PHONY: docker-push
docker-push: ## Push workload manager image.
	$(CONTAINER_TOOL) push $(WORKLOAD_MANAGER_IMAGE)

.PHONY: kind-load
kind-load: ## Load workload manager image into Kind.
	kind load docker-image $(WORKLOAD_MANAGER_IMAGE) --name $(E2E_CLUSTER_NAME)

.PHONY: kind-load-router
kind-load-router: ## Load router image into Kind.
	kind load docker-image $(ROUTER_IMAGE) --name $(E2E_CLUSTER_NAME)

##@ E2E

.PHONY: e2e
e2e: ## Run E2E tests.
	./test/e2e/run_e2e.sh

.PHONY: e2e-clean
e2e-clean: ## Clean up E2E resources.
	kind delete cluster --name $(E2E_CLUSTER_NAME) || true
	rm -rf /tmp/agent-sandbox || true

##@ Python SDK

.PHONY: build-python-sdk
build-python-sdk: ## Build Python SDK package.
	cp LICENSE sdk-python/
	cd sdk-python && python3 -m build
	rm sdk-python/LICENSE

##@ Tools

.PHONY: controller-gen
controller-gen: ## Download controller-gen locally if necessary.
	$(call go-install-tool,$(CONTROLLER_GEN),sigs.k8s.io/controller-tools/cmd/controller-gen,$(CONTROLLER_TOOLS_VERSION))

.PHONY: golangci-lint
golangci-lint: ## Download golangci-lint locally if necessary.
	$(call go-install-tool,$(GOLANGCI_LINT),github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/cmd/golangci-lint,$(GOLANGCI_LINT_VERSION))

define go-install-tool
@[ -f "$(1)-$(3)" ] || { \
set -e; \
package=$(2) ;\
version=$(3) ;\
tool_install_dir=$$(dirname "$(1)") ;\
mkdir -p "$${tool_install_dir}" ;\
tmp_dir=$$(mktemp -d) ;\
cd "$${tmp_dir}" ;\
go install "$${package}@$${version}" ;\
mv "$${GOPATH}/bin/$$(basename "$${package}")" "$(1)-$(3)" ;\
ln -sf "$(1)-$(3)" "$(1)" ;\
rm -rf "$${tmp_dir}" ;\
}
endef

.PHONY: install
install: build ## Install workload manager binary.
	sudo cp bin/workloadmanager /usr/local/bin/

# AgentCube Implementation Plan

## Overview

This document provides a detailed implementation plan for Architectural Requirements AR-034 through AR-043, mapping to the specification documents in `./specs/`. Each AR includes file paths, precise logic, and implementation details.

---

## AR-034: CI/CD Workflows - Additional GitHub Actions

**Spec Reference:** `specs/ci-cd/spec.md`, `specs/ci-cd/design.md`

### Status: PARTIAL - Missing Workflows

### Files to Create:

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `.github/workflows/codegen-check.yml` | Verify generated code matches `make gen-all` |
| `.github/workflows/copyright-check.yml` | Ensure copyright headers are present |
| `.github/workflows/codespell.yml` | Spelling check with codespell |
| `.github/workflows/build-push-release.yml` | Multi-arch image push to GHCR |
| `.github/workflows/dify-plugin-publish.yml` | Publish Dify plugin on tag |
| `.github/workflows/workflows-approve.yml` | Approve first-time contributor workflows |

### Key Logic:

**codegen-check.yml:**
```yaml
name: Codegen Check

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, 'release-*']

jobs:
  codegen-check:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@v3
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            codegen:
              - 'pkg/apis/**'
              - 'hack/**'
              - '.github/workflows/**'
              - 'Makefile'
      - uses: actions/setup-go@v5
        if: steps.filter.outputs.codegen == 'true'
        with:
          go-version: '1.24.4'
      - run: make gen-check
        if: steps.filter.outputs.codegen == 'true'
```

**copyright-check.yml:**
```yaml
name: Copyright Check

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, 'release-*']

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: dorny/paths-filter@v3
        id: filter
        with:
          filters: |
            copyright:
              - '**'
              - '!**/*.md'
              - '!**/*.svg'
              - '!**/*.png'
              - '!docs/**'
              - '!.github/**'
      - run: sudo apt-get install -y moreutils
        if: steps.filter.outputs.copyright == 'true'
      - run: make gen-copyright
        if: steps.filter.outputs.copyright == 'true'
      - run: git diff --exit-code
        if: steps.filter.outputs.copyright == 'true'
```

**codespell.yml:**
```yaml
name: Codespell

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, 'release-*']

jobs:
  codespell:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Backup files causing false positives
        run: |
          find . -name 'pyproject.toml' -exec mv {} {}.bak \;
          find . -name 'package-lock.json' -exec mv {} {}.bak \;
          find . -name 'package.json' -exec mv {} {}.bak \;
      - run: pip install codespell
      - run: |
          codespell --check-filenames \
            --skip=".git,*.pyc,*.svg,*.png,*.jpg,*.gif,*.ico,go.sum" \
            --ignore-words-list="fo,nam,te,notin,NotIn" .
      - name: Restore files
        if: always()
        run: |
          find . -name '*.bak' -exec sh -c 'mv "$1" "${1%.bak}"' _ {} \;
```

**build-push-release.yml:**
```yaml
name: Build and Push Release Images

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ['v*.*.*', 'v*.*.*-*']

jobs:
  build-and-push:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      packages: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-go@v4
        with:
          go-version: '1.24.4'
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v2
      - uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - name: Set TAG
        run: |
          if [[ "${{ github.ref_type }}" == "tag" ]]; then
            echo "TAG=${{ github.ref_name }}" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          else
            echo "TAG=latest" >> $GITHUB_ENV
          fi
      - run: make docker-buildx-push
        env:
          IMAGE_REGISTRY: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}
          WORKLOAD_MANAGER_IMAGE: workloadmanager:${{ env.TAG }}
      - run: make docker-buildx-push-router
        env:
          IMAGE_REGISTRY: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}
          ROUTER_IMAGE: agentcube-router:${{ env.TAG }}
      - run: make docker-buildx-push-picod
        env:
          IMAGE_REGISTRY: ghcr.io/${{ github.repository_owner }}
          PICOD_IMAGE: picod:${{ env.TAG }}
```

**dify-plugin-publish.yml:**
```yaml
name: Dify Plugin Publish

on:
  push:
    tags: ['dify-plugin/v*']

jobs:
  publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.12'
      - run: pip install dify-plugin==0.0.6 yq
      - name: Read manifest
        id: manifest
        run: |
          echo "author=$(yq .author integrations/dify-plugin/manifest.yaml)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          echo "name=$(yq .name integrations/dify-plugin/manifest.yaml)" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      - run: |
          cd integrations/dify-plugin
          dify-plugin package
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          repository: ${{ steps.manifest.outputs.author }}/dify-plugins
          token: ${{ secrets.PLUGIN_ACTION }}
          path: dify-plugins
      - run: |
          mv integrations/dify-plugin/*.difypkg dify-plugins/
          cd dify-plugins
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add .
          git commit -m "Add ${{ steps.manifest.outputs.name }} plugin"
          git push origin HEAD:plugin/${{ steps.manifest.outputs.name }}
      - run: |
          gh pr create \
            --repo langgenius/dify-plugins \
            --head "${{ steps.manifest.outputs.author }}:plugin/${{ steps.manifest.outputs.name }}" \
            --title "Add ${{ steps.manifest.outputs.name }} plugin" \
            --body "Automated plugin publish"
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PLUGIN_ACTION }}
```

**workflows-approve.yml:**
```yaml
name: Approve Workflows

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [labeled, synchronize]
    branches: [main, 'release-**']

jobs:
  approve:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: |
      github.event.action == 'labeled' && github.event.label.name == 'ok-to-test' ||
      github.event.action == 'synchronize'
    steps:
      - uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const { owner, repo } = context.repo;
            const sha = context.payload.pull_request.head.sha;
            
            const prs = await github.rest.pulls.list({
              owner,
              repo,
              state: 'all',
              creator: context.payload.pull_request.user.login
            });
            
            const isFirstTime = prs.data.length <= 1;
            const hasOkToTest = context.payload.pull_request.labels.some(l => l.name === 'ok-to-test');
            
            if (!isFirstTime || hasOkToTest) {
              const runs = await github.rest.actions.listWorkflowRunsForRepo({
                owner,
                repo,
                head_sha: sha,
                status: 'action_required'
              });
              
              for (const run of runs.data.workflow_runs) {
                await github.rest.actions.approveWorkflowRun({
                  owner,
                  repo,
                  run_id: run.id
                });
                core.info(`Approved workflow run ${run.id}`);
              }
            }
```

**templates/workloadmanager.yaml:**
```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata:
  name: workloadmanager
  namespace: {{ .Release.Namespace }}
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: workloadmanager
  namespace: {{ .Release.Namespace }}
  labels:
    app: workloadmanager
spec:
  replicas: {{ .Values.workloadmanager.replicas }}
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: workloadmanager
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: workloadmanager
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: workloadmanager
      {{- with .Values.imagePullSecrets }}
      imagePullSecrets:
        {{- toYaml . | nindent 8 }}
      {{- end }}
      containers:
        - name: workloadmanager
          image: "{{ .Values.workloadmanager.image.repository }}:{{ .Values.workloadmanager.image.tag }}"
          imagePullPolicy: {{ .Values.workloadmanager.image.pullPolicy }}
          ports:
            - name: http
              containerPort: {{ .Values.workloadmanager.service.port }}
              protocol: TCP
          env:
            - name: AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE
              valueFrom:
                fieldRef:
                  fieldPath: metadata.namespace
            - name: REDIS_ADDR
              value: {{ .Values.redis.addr }}
            - name: REDIS_PASSWORD
              value: {{ .Values.redis.password }}
            {{- with .Values.workloadmanager.extraEnv }}
            {{- toYaml . | nindent 12 }}
            {{- end }}
          args:
            - --port={{ .Values.workloadmanager.service.port }}
            - --runtime-class-name=
          resources:
            {{- toYaml .Values.workloadmanager.resources | nindent 12 }}
          livenessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health
              port: http
            initialDelaySeconds: 10
            periodSeconds: 10
          readinessProbe:
            httpGet:
              path: /health
              port: http
            initialDelaySeconds: 5
            periodSeconds: 5
---

metadata:
  name: workloadmanager
  namespace: {{ .Release.Namespace }}
spec:
  type: {{ .Values.workloadmanager.service.type }}
  ports:
    - port: {{ .Values.workloadmanager.service.port }}
      targetPort: http
      protocol: TCP
      name: http
  selector:
    app: workloadmanager

## AR-036: Docker Build Infrastructure

**Spec Reference:** `specs/deployment/spec.md`, `specs/deployment/design.md`

### Status: NOT IMPLEMENTED

### Files to Create:

| File | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `docker/Dockerfile` | Workload Manager image (multi-stage Go build) |
| `docker/Dockerfile.router` | Router image |
| `docker/Dockerfile.picod` | PicoD image |

### Key Logic:

**docker/Dockerfile (Workload Manager):**
```dockerfile
# Build stage
FROM golang:1.24.9-alpine AS builder

WORKDIR /workspace

RUN apk add --no-cache git

COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download

COPY cmd/ pkg/ ./
RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build \
    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=${TARGETARCH:-amd64} \
    go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o workloadmanager ./cmd/workload-manager

# Runtime stage
FROM alpine:3.19

RUN apk add --no-cache ca-certificates

WORKDIR /app

COPY --from=builder /workspace/workloadmanager .

RUN adduser -D -u 1000 apiserver

USER apiserver

EXPOSE 8080

ENTRYPOINT ["/app/workloadmanager"]

CMD ["--port=8080"]
```

**docker/Dockerfile.router:**
```dockerfile

WORKDIR /workspace

RUN apk add --no-cache git

COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download

RUN --mount=type=cache,target=/root/.cache/go-build \
    CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=${TARGETARCH:-amd64} \
    go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o agentcube-router ./cmd/router

# Runtime stage
FROM alpine:3.19

RUN apk add --no-cache ca-certificates

WORKDIR /app

COPY --from=builder /workspace/agentcube-router .

RUN adduser -D -u 1000 router

USER router

**docker/Dockerfile.picod:**
```dockerfile
# Build stage
FROM golang:1.24.4 AS builder

WORKDIR /app

COPY go.mod go.sum ./
RUN go mod download

COPY . .
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o picod ./cmd/picod

# Runtime stage
FROM ubuntu:24.04

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y python3 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /root/

from typing import Any
from dify_plugin import ToolProvider
from dify_plugin.errors.tool import ToolProviderCredentialValidationError


class AgentcubeCodeInterpreterProvider(ToolProvider):

        router_url = credentials.get("router_url")
        workload_manager_url = credentials.get("workload_manager_url")
        
        if not router_url:
            raise ToolProviderCredentialValidationError("router_url is required")
        if not workload_manager_url:
            raise ToolProviderCredentialValidationError("workload_manager_url is required")

```python
from collections.abc import Generator
from typing import Any

from dify_plugin import Tool
from dify_plugin.entities.tool import ToolInvokeMessage

import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '/app/agentcube-sdk')
from agentcube import CodeInterpreterClient


class AgentcubeCodeInterpreterTool(Tool):
    def _invoke(self, tool_parameters: dict[str, Any]) -> Generator[ToolInvokeMessage]:
        result = self.execute(**tool_parameters)
        yield self.create_json_message(result)

    def execute(
        self,
        router_url: str = None,
        workload_manager_url: str = None,
        language: str = "python",
        code_interpreter_id: str = None,
        session_id: str = None,
        code: str = None,
        command: str = None,
        session_reuse: bool = False,
        **kwargs
    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
        result = {
            "success": False,
            "output": "",
            "error": None,
            "session_id": None,
        }
        
        effective_session_id = session_id if session_reuse else None
        
        try:
            with CodeInterpreterClient(
                router_url=router_url,
                workload_manager_url=workload_manager_url,
                session_id=effective_session_id,
                code_interpreter_name=code_interpreter_id,
            ) as client:
                if code:
                    exec_result = client.run_code(code, language=language)
                    result["output"] = exec_result.get("stdout", "")
                    result["error"] = exec_result.get("stderr", "") if exec_result.get("exit_code", 0) != 0 else None
                    result["success"] = exec_result.get("exit_code", 0) == 0
                elif command:
                    exec_result = client.execute_command(command)
                    result["output"] = exec_result.get("stdout", "")
                    result["error"] = exec_result.get("stderr", "") if exec_result.get("exit_code", 0) != 0 else None
                    result["success"] = exec_result.get("exit_code", 0) == 0
                
                result["session_id"] = client.session_id
                
        except Exception as e:
            result["error"] = str(e)
            result["success"] = False
        
        return result
```

| `example/pcap-analyzer/deployment.yaml` | Kubernetes Deployment |
| `example/pcap-analyzer/README.md` | Documentation |

### Key Logic:

**pcap_analyzer.py:**
```python
import os
import sys
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional

from fastapi import FastAPI, File, Form, HTTPException, UploadFile
from pydantic import BaseModel
import uvicorn

sys.path.insert(0, '/app/agentcube')
from agentcube import CodeInterpreterClient
from agentcube.exceptions import CommandExecutionError

API_KEY = os.getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY")
API_BASE_URL = os.getenv("OPENAI_API_BASE", "https://api.siliconflow.cn/v1")
MODEL_NAME = os.getenv("OPENAI_MODEL", "Qwen/QwQ-32B")
CODEINTERPRETER_NAME = os.getenv("CODEINTERPRETER_NAME", "my-interpreter")
SANDBOX_NAMESPACE = os.getenv("SANDBOX_NAMESPACE", "default")
SANDBOX_WARMUP_SEC = int(os.getenv("SANDBOX_WARMUP_SEC", "5"))

SERVER_HOST = "0.0.0.0"
SERVER_PORT = 8000
SERVER_RELOAD = True

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langgraph.prebuilt import create_react_agent
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, AIMessage


class SandboxRunner:
    def __init__(self, name: str = "my-interpreter", namespace: str = "default", warmup_sec: int = 5):
        self.name = name
        self.namespace = namespace
        self.warmup_sec = warmup_sec
        self.client: Optional[CodeInterpreterClient] = None
    
    def _ensure_client(self, router_url: str, workload_manager_url: str):
        if self.client is None:
            self.client = CodeInterpreterClient(
                router_url=router_url,
                workload_manager_url=workload_manager_url,
                code_interpreter_name=self.name,
            )
    
    def upload_file(self, local_path: str, remote_path: str, router_url: str, workload_manager_url: str) -> bool:
        self._ensure_client(router_url, workload_manager_url)
        try:
            self.client.upload_file(local_path, remote_path)
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Upload failed: {e}")
            return False
    
    def upload_bytes(self, data: bytes, remote_path: str, router_url: str, workload_manager_url: str) -> bool:
        self._ensure_client(router_url, workload_manager_url)
        try:
            self.client.write_file(remote_path, data.decode('utf-8', errors='replace'))
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Upload bytes failed: {e}")
            return False
    
    def run(self, command: str, router_url: str, workload_manager_url: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        self._ensure_client(router_url, workload_manager_url)
        try:
            result = self.client.execute_command(command)
            return {
                "stdout": result.get("stdout", ""),
                "stderr": result.get("stderr", ""),
                "exit_code": result.get("exit_code", 0),
            }
        except CommandExecutionError as e:
            return {
                "stdout": "",
                "stderr": str(e),
                "exit_code": e.exit_code,
            }
    
    def stop(self):
        if self.client:
            self.client.stop()
            self.client = None


def build_react_agent(llm, system_prompt: str):
    return create_react_agent(llm, [], state_modifier=system_prompt)


def invoke_react_agent(agent, user_text: str) -> str:
    result = agent.invoke({"messages": [HumanMessage(content=user_text)]})
    messages = result.get("messages", [])
    for msg in reversed(messages):
        if isinstance(msg, AIMessage):
            return msg.content
    return ""


def build_planner_agent(llm):
    return build_react_agent(
        llm,
        "You are a network security expert. Given a PCAP file, write Python scripts using scapy to analyze it."
    )


def build_reporter_agent(llm):
    return build_react_agent(
        llm,
        "You are a security analyst. Given execution results from PCAP analysis tools, write a comprehensive security report."
    )


class AnalyzeResponse(BaseModel):
    script: str
    results: List[Dict[str, Any]]
    report: str


PLANNER = None
REPORTER = None
ROUTER_URL = os.getenv("ROUTER_URL", "")
WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL = os.getenv("WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL", "")


def on_startup():
    global PLANNER, REPORTER
    if not API_KEY:
        raise RuntimeError("OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable is required")
    
    llm = ChatOpenAI(
        api_key=API_KEY,
        base_url=API_BASE_URL,
        model=MODEL_NAME,
    )
    PLANNER = build_planner_agent(llm)
    REPORTER = build_reporter_agent(llm)
    print("Startup complete: LLM agents initialized")


app = FastAPI(
    title="PCAP Analyzer",
    on_startup=[on_startup],
)


@app.post("/analyze", response_model=AnalyzeResponse)
async def analyze_endpoint(
    pcap_file: Optional[UploadFile] = File(None),
    pcap_path: Optional[str] = Form(None),
):
    if not pcap_file and not pcap_path:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Either pcap_file or pcap_path is required")
    
    runner = SandboxRunner(
        name=CODEINTERPRETER_NAME,
        namespace=SANDBOX_NAMESPACE,
        warmup_sec=SANDBOX_WARMUP_SEC,
    )
    
    try:
        if pcap_file:
            pcap_bytes = await pcap_file.read()
            remote_pcap = f"/tmp/{pcap_file.filename}"
            runner.upload_bytes(pcap_bytes, remote_pcap, ROUTER_URL, WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL)
            local_pcap = remote_pcap
        else:
            local_pcap = pcap_path
        
        script = invoke_react_agent(PLANNER, f"Analyze PCAP file at {local_pcap}")
        
        results = []
        exec_result = runner.run(f"python3 -c '{script}'", ROUTER_URL, WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL)
        results.append(exec_result)
        
        if exec_result.get("exit_code", 0) != 0:
            repaired = invoke_react_agent(
                PLANNER,
                f"Previous script failed with error: {exec_result.get('stderr')}. Fix it."
            )
            exec_result = runner.run(f"python3 -c '{repaired}'", ROUTER_URL, WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL)
            results.append(exec_result)
            script = repaired
        
        report = invoke_react_agent(REPORTER, f"Write a report based on: {results}")
        
        return AnalyzeResponse(script=script, results=results, report=report)
    
    finally:
        runner.stop()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    uvicorn.run(app, host=SERVER_HOST, port=SERVER_PORT, reload=SERVER_RELOAD)
```

### Key Logic:

**run_e2e.sh:**
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

E2E_CLUSTER_NAME="${E2E_CLUSTER_NAME:-agentcube-e2e}"
E2E_SKIP_SETUP="${E2E_SKIP_SETUP:-false}"
E2E_CLEAN_CLUSTER="${E2E_CLEAN_CLUSTER:-true}"
E2E_VENV_DIR="${E2E_VENV_DIR:-/tmp/agentcube-e2e-venv}"
AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE="${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE:-agentcube}"
AGENT_SANDBOX_VERSION="${AGENT_SANDBOX_VERSION:-v0.1.1}"
WORKLOAD_MANAGER_LOCAL_PORT="${WORKLOAD_MANAGER_LOCAL_PORT:-8080}"
ROUTER_LOCAL_PORT="${ROUTER_LOCAL_PORT:-8081}"
REDIS_IMAGE="${REDIS_IMAGE:-redis:7-alpine}"

ARTIFACTS_PATH="${ARTIFACTS_PATH:-/tmp/agentcube-e2e-artifacts}"
mkdir -p "${ARTIFACTS_PATH}"

run_setup() {
    echo "=== Setting up E2E environment ==="
    
    if kind get clusters | grep -q "${E2E_CLUSTER_NAME}"; then
        if [[ "${E2E_CLEAN_CLUSTER}" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "Deleting existing cluster..."
            kind delete cluster --name "${E2E_CLUSTER_NAME}"
        else
            echo "Cluster already exists, skipping creation"
            return
        fi
    fi
    
    echo "Creating Kind cluster..."
    kind create cluster --name "${E2E_CLUSTER_NAME}"
    
    echo "Installing agent-sandbox CRDs..."
    kubectl apply --validate=false -f "https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox/releases/download/${AGENT_SANDBOX_VERSION}/manifest.yaml"
    kubectl apply --validate=false -f "https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/agent-sandbox/releases/download/${AGENT_SANDBOX_VERSION}/extensions.yaml"
    
    echo "Pulling and loading images..."
    docker pull "registry.k8s.io/agent-sandbox/agent-sandbox-controller:${AGENT_SANDBOX_VERSION}"
    docker pull python:3.9-slim
    kind load docker-image "registry.k8s.io/agent-sandbox/agent-sandbox-controller:${AGENT_SANDBOX_VERSION}" --name "${E2E_CLUSTER_NAME}"
    kind load docker-image python:3.9-slim --name "${E2E_CLUSTER_NAME}"
    
    echo "Building local images..."
    make docker-build docker-build-router docker-build-picod
    kind load docker-image workloadmanager:latest agentcube-router:latest picod:latest --name "${E2E_CLUSTER_NAME}"
    
    echo "Deploying Redis..."
    kubectl create namespace "${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}" || true
    kubectl apply -n "${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}" -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: redis
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: redis
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: redis
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: redis
          image: ${REDIS_IMAGE}
          ports:
            - containerPort: 6379
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: redis
spec:
  selector:
    app: redis
  ports:
    - port: 6379
      targetPort: 6379
EOF
    
    echo "Waiting for Redis..."
    kubectl wait --for=condition=available --timeout=60s deployment/redis -n "${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}"
    
    echo "Deploying AgentCube with Helm..."
    helm upgrade --install agentcube manifests/charts/base \
        --namespace "${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}" \
        --create-namespace \
        --set redis.addr="redis.${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}.svc.cluster.local:6379" \
        --set redis.password="" \
        --set workloadmanager.image.repository="workloadmanager" \
        --set workloadmanager.image.tag="latest" \
        --set-json 'workloadmanager.extraEnv=[{"name":"REDIS_PASSWORD_REQUIRED","value":"false"},{"name":"JWT_KEY_SECRET_NAMESPACE","value":"agentcube"}]' \
        --set router.image.repository="agentcube-router" \
        --set router.image.tag="latest" \
        --set router.rbac.create=true \
        --set router.serviceAccountName="agentcube-router" \
        --set-json 'router.extraEnv=[{"name":"REDIS_PASSWORD_REQUIRED","value":"false"}]' \
        --wait
    
    echo "Applying test fixtures..."
    kubectl apply -f test/e2e/echo_agent.yaml
    sed 's/sessionTimeout: "15m"/sessionTimeout: "30s"/' test/e2e/echo_agent.yaml | kubectl apply -f -
    kubectl apply -f test/e2e/e2e_code_interpreter.yaml
    
    echo "Setting up port forwards..."
    kubectl port-forward svc/workloadmanager -n "${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}" ${WORKLOAD_MANAGER_LOCAL_PORT}:8080 &
    WORKLOAD_PF_PID=$!
    kubectl port-forward svc/agentcube-router -n "${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}" ${ROUTER_LOCAL_PORT}:8080 &
    ROUTER_PF_PID=$!
    
    sleep 5
    
    echo "Waiting for health endpoints..."
    curl -s --retry 10 --retry-delay 2 "http://localhost:${WORKLOAD_MANAGER_LOCAL_PORT}/health" || true
    curl -s --retry 10 --retry-delay 2 "http://localhost:${ROUTER_LOCAL_PORT}/health/live" || true
    
    echo "Creating test service account..."
    kubectl create serviceaccount e2e-test -n "${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}" || true
    kubectl create clusterrolebinding e2e-test-binding --clusterrole=workloadmanager --serviceaccount="${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}:e2e-test" || true
    export API_TOKEN=$(kubectl create token e2e-test -n "${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}" --duration=24h)
    
    echo "Setting up Python venv..."
    python3 -m venv "${E2E_VENV_DIR}"
    "${E2E_VENV_DIR}/bin/pip" install -e ./sdk-python
    
    echo "Setup complete!"
}

run_tests() {
    echo "=== Running E2E tests ==="
    
    export WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL="http://localhost:${WORKLOAD_MANAGER_LOCAL_PORT}"
    export ROUTER_URL="http://localhost:${ROUTER_LOCAL_PORT}"
    export API_TOKEN
    export AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE
    
    echo "Running Go tests..."
    go test -v ./test/e2e/...
    
    echo "Running Python tests..."
    cd test/e2e
    "${E2E_VENV_DIR}/bin/python" test_codeinterpreter.py --verbose
    cd -
}

collect_logs() {
    echo "Collecting logs to ${ARTIFACTS_PATH}..."
    kubectl logs -n "${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}" -l app=workloadmanager --tail=1000 > "${ARTIFACTS_PATH}/workloadmanager.log" 2>&1 || true
    kubectl logs -n "${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}" -l app=agentcube-router --tail=1000 > "${ARTIFACTS_PATH}/router.log" 2>&1 || true
    kubectl get pods -n "${AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE}" -o wide > "${ARTIFACTS_PATH}/pods.txt" 2>&1 || true
}

cleanup() {
    echo "=== Cleaning up ==="
    kind delete cluster --name "${E2E_CLUSTER_NAME}" || true
    rm -rf /tmp/agent-sandbox || true
}

main() {
    if [[ "${E2E_SKIP_SETUP}" != "true" ]]; then
        run_setup
    fi
    
    if run_tests; then
        echo "All tests passed!"
        exit 0
    else
        echo "Tests failed!"
        collect_logs
        exit 1
    fi
}

trap cleanup EXIT
main
```

**e2e_test.go:**
```go
package e2e

import (
	"context"
	"os"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestMain(m *testing.M) {
	os.Exit(m.Run())
}

func TestWorkloadManagerHealth(t *testing.T) {
	url := os.Getenv("WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL")
	require.NotEmpty(t, url, "WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL must be set")
	
	client := NewTestClient(url, os.Getenv("API_TOKEN"))
	
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
	defer cancel()
	
	health, err := client.Health(ctx)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "healthy", health.Status)
}

func TestRouterHealth(t *testing.T) {
	url := os.Getenv("ROUTER_URL")
	require.NotEmpty(t, url, "ROUTER_URL must be set")
	
	client := NewTestClient(url, "")
	
	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
	defer cancel()
	
	health, err := client.Live(ctx)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "alive", health.Status)
}

func TestCreateAndInvokeAgentRuntime(t *testing.T) {
	wmURL := os.Getenv("WORKLOAD_MANAGER_URL")
	routerURL := os.Getenv("ROUTER_URL")
	token := os.Getenv("API_TOKEN")
	namespace := os.Getenv("AGENTCUBE_NAMESPACE")
	
	require.NotEmpty(t, wmURL)
	require.NotEmpty(t, routerURL)
	require.NotEmpty(t, namespace)
	
	wmClient := NewTestClient(wmURL, token)
	routerClient := NewTestClient(routerURL, "")
	
	ctx := context.Background()
	
	resp, err := wmClient.CreateSandbox(ctx, &CreateSandboxRequest{
		Kind:      "AgentRuntime",
		Name:      "echo-agent",
		Namespace: namespace,
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.NotEmpty(t, resp.SessionID)
	
	defer wmClient.DeleteSandbox(ctx, resp.SessionID)
	
	invokeResp, err := routerClient.Invoke(ctx, namespace, "echo-agent", map[string]interface{}{
		"message": "hello",
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, invokeResp, "message")
}
```

define go-install-tool
@[ -f "$(1)-$(3)" ] || { \
set -e; \
package=$(2) ;\
version=$(3) ;\
tool_install_dir=$$(dirname "$(1)") ;\
mkdir -p "$${tool_install_dir}" ;\
tmp_dir=$$(mktemp -d) ;\
cd "$${tmp_dir}" ;\
go install "$${package}@$${version}" ;\
mv "$${GOPATH}/bin/$$(basename "$${package}")" "$(1)-$(3)" ;\
ln -sf "$(1)-$(3)" "$(1)" ;\
rm -rf "$${tmp_dir}" ;\
}
endef

.PHONY: install
install: build ## Install workload manager binary.
	sudo cp bin/workloadmanager /usr/local/bin/

## AR-041: Hack Scripts (Code Generation and Copyright)

**Spec Reference:** `specs/deployment/design.md`

| `hack/boilerplate.py.txt` | Python file copyright template |

### Key Logic:

**hack/update-codegen.sh:**
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_ROOT=$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/..
CODEGEN_PKG=${CODEGEN_PKG:-$(cd "${SCRIPT_ROOT}" && ls -d -1 ./vendor/k8s.io/code-generator 2>/dev/null || echo ../k8s.io/code-generator)}

source "${CODEGEN_PKG}/kube_codegen.sh"

# Generate client for runtime/v1alpha1
kube::codegen::gen_client \
    --with-watch \
    --output-dir "${SCRIPT_ROOT}/client-go" \
    --output-pkg "github.com/volcano-sh/agentcube/client-go" \
    --boilerplate "${SCRIPT_ROOT}/hack/boilerplate.go.txt" \
    --one-input-api "runtime/v1alpha1" \
    "${SCRIPT_ROOT}/pkg/apis"

# Post-process lister files to add GroupResource method
for file in client-go/listers/runtime/v1alpha1/agentruntime.go client-go/listers/runtime/v1alpha1/codeinterpreter.go; do
    if [[ -f "${file}" ]]; then
        resource_name=$(basename "${file}" .go)
        if [[ "${resource_name}" == "agentruntime" ]]; then
            resource="agentruntimes"
        else
            resource="codeinterpreters"
        fi
        echo "
// GroupResource returns the group resource of the ${resource_name}.
func (*${resource_name^}) GroupResource() schema.GroupResource {
    return schema.GroupResource{Group: \"runtime.agentcube.volcano.sh\", Resource: \"${resource}\"}
}" >> "${file}"
    fi
done

echo "Code generation complete."
```

**hack/update-copyright.sh:**
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_ROOT=$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/..
GO_BOILERPLATE="${SCRIPT_ROOT}/hack/boilerplate.go.txt"
PY_BOILERPLATE="${SCRIPT_ROOT}/hack/boilerplate.py.txt"

GO_HEADER=$(cat "${GO_BOILERPLATE}")
PY_HEADER=$(cat "${PY_BOILERPLATE}")

# Process Go files
find "${SCRIPT_ROOT}" -name "*.go" -not -path "*/vendor/*" -not -path "*/venv/*" | while read -r file; do
    if ! grep -q "Copyright The Volcano Authors" "${file}"; then
        echo "Adding copyright to ${file}"
        echo "${GO_HEADER}" | cat - "${file}" > "${file}.tmp" && mv "${file}.tmp" "${file}"
    fi
done

# Process Python files
find "${SCRIPT_ROOT}" -name "*.py" -not -path "*/vendor/*" -not -path "*/venv/*" -not -path "*/__pycache__/*" | while read -r file; do
    if ! grep -q "Copyright The Volcano Authors" "${file}"; then
        echo "Adding copyright to ${file}"
        echo "${PY_HEADER}" | cat - "${file}" > "${file}.tmp" && mv "${file}.tmp" "${file}"
    fi
done

echo "Copyright headers updated."
```

### Key Logic:

**README.md:**
```markdown
# AgentCube

AgentCube is a Kubernetes-native sandbox orchestration platform for AI agents.

## Overview

AgentCube provisions isolated sandbox pods on demand, routes HTTP traffic to them through session-aware proxies, and manages their lifecycle (creation, expiry, idle cleanup).

## Features

- **Sandbox Orchestration**: Kubernetes CRDs for AgentRuntime and CodeInterpreter
- **Session Routing**: HTTP proxy with JWT authentication
- **Idle Cleanup**: Automatic cleanup of inactive sandboxes
- **Python SDK**: Easy-to-use client library
- **CLI Toolkit**: Developer-friendly command-line interface

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- Kubernetes 1.28+
- Helm 3.x
- Redis/Valkey

### Install with Helm

```bash
helm upgrade --install agentcube manifests/charts/base \
  --namespace agentcube --create-namespace \
  --set redis.addr="redis.agentcube.svc.cluster.local:6379" \
  --set redis.password=""
```

### Using the Python SDK

```python
from agentcube import CodeInterpreterClient

with CodeInterpreterClient(
    router_url="http://router.example.com:8080",
    workload_manager_url="http://workloadmanager.example.com:8080"
) as client:
    result = client.run_code("print('Hello, AgentCube!')")
    print(result["stdout"])
```

## Components

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Workload Manager | Sandbox creation and lifecycle management |
| Router | Session-aware HTTP proxy |
| PicoD | Code execution daemon |
| AgentD | Idle cleanup controller |
| Python SDK | Client library for AgentCube |
| CLI | Developer toolkit |

## Documentation

- [Getting Started](docs/getting-started.md)
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md)
- [API Reference](docs/api-reference.md)
- [CLI Reference](docs/cli-reference.md)

## License

Apache 2.0
```
